note: Computed Field adds read-only fields whose value is generated by a `computed_field` plugin that declares a core field type and implements computeValue(). A configured field is stored as a `computed_field` config entity (id entity_type.bundle.field_name) holding plugin_id + plugin_config; the module ships one usable plugin, `reverse_entity_reference` (field type entity_reference). Recipe cases are response-graded; medium cases add a known computed_field entity and read it back from live config; hard cases build a computed field on a content type and verify the config entity + resolved field type on the live site. Admin UI is in the computed_field_ui submodule (its own evals.json).

evals.prompt: On a Drupal 11 site using the contrib `computed_field` module, where does a computed field's value come from, and is the value stored in the database? What core concept produces it?
evals.expected_output: The value is generated dynamically at runtime by a computed field plugin's computeValue() method; nothing is stored in the database. The plugin declares a core field type so existing field formatters can render the value. Computed fields are read-only.

evals.prompt: In the `computed_field` module, when I write a computed field plugin, what does its `field_type` attribute property refer to, and give examples? Does the module invent its own field types?
evals.expected_output: field_type is a normal CORE field type id (e.g. string, integer, entity_reference, text, link, datetime). The module does not invent field types — it reuses core ones so existing formatters/settings apply. No data is stored; the type only chooses display.

evals.prompt: Which ready-to-use computed field plugin ships with the `computed_field` module out of the box, what core field type does it use, and what does its configuration (`reference_field`) look like?
evals.expected_output: reverse_entity_reference — an entity_reference computed field listing entities that reference the host. Its reference_field config is in the form HOST_ENTITY_TYPE-FIELD_NAME (e.g. node-uid); computeValue() queries all entities whose field points at the host.

evals.prompt: This Drupal site has a computed field named `computed_eval_backlinks` on the Article content type. Inspect the live site and report which computed field plugin supplies its value and what core field type the field resolves to.
evals.expected_output: The computed_field config entity node.article.computed_eval_backlinks uses plugin_id reverse_entity_reference and resolves to core field type entity_reference (getType()).

evals.prompt: On this live Drupal site there is a computed field `computed_eval_authored` on Article nodes, using the reverse entity reference plugin. What is the exact `reference_field` value stored in its plugin configuration?
evals.expected_output: The plugin_config of node.article.computed_eval_authored has reference_field = node-field_eval_source (format HOST_ENTITY_TYPE-FIELD_NAME).

evals.prompt: On this Drupal site, add a computed field to the Article content type using the computed_field module's bundled `reverse_entity_reference` plugin. Use the field machine name `computed_eval_referrers` and configure its `reference_field` as `node-uid`. Apply it to the live site so the computed field exists on Article nodes.
evals.expected_output: A computed_field config entity node.article.computed_eval_referrers now exists, with plugin_id reverse_entity_reference and resolving to core field type entity_reference.

evals.prompt: Add a read-only computed field to the Basic page content type on this Drupal site. It must be supplied by the computed_field module's `reverse_entity_reference` plugin, have machine name `computed_eval_pagerefs`, and use `reference_field` = `node-uid`. Persist it to the live site.
evals.expected_output: A computed_field config entity node.page.computed_eval_pagerefs now exists, with plugin_id reverse_entity_reference and resolving to core field type entity_reference.
